title: Flaky Ethiopian Almond Dulet
description: Herbal almond crumble with delicate parsley spheres, perfect for a date-night Ethiopian-inspired dish.
instructions: Spread the almonds on a baking sheet and toast for 8 minutes until fragrant.
Let the almonds cool, then pulse in a food processor until coarsely ground.
In a bowl combine the ground almonds, chopped parsley, smoked paprika, cumin, sea salt, pepper, and date syrup.
Dissolve the sodium alginate in 200ml filtered water, shaking vigorously for 5 minutes.
Prepare a calcium chloride solution by dissolving 0.5g calcium chloride in 100ml filtered water.
Drop 2 teaspoons of the almond mixture into the alginate bath using a pipette to form small spheres; let them sit 30 seconds.
Remove the spheres with a slotted spoon and rinse briefly in the calcium solution.
Gently fold the spheres into the remaining almond mixture to coat.
Transfer the mixture to a parchment-lined dish and bake at 350°F for 12 minutes until flaky.
Let the dish cool for 5 minutes before serving warm.
